message.content: Olá! Estamos implementando um orquestrador de Harness na pasta ./packages/harness-orchestrator - verifique os steps salvos dentro dele e execute o harness dentro dele, ou seja, salvando os status dele dentro dele e comitando as coisas dele separadamente. Para iniciar, dentro dele, veja o arquivo docs/PLAN-O022-DOCTOR.md e de forma adequada, usando 'deep thinking', crie tasks de forma adequada para a implementacao do 'harness doctor' descrito no arquivo e depois exclua o arquivo - como o arquivo será excluido, inclua todo o contexto necessário no arquivo de tarefas, de modo que o agent executer não tenha nenhuma dúvida do que deve ser feito erro por  possíveis pontos de desentendimento.

content.text: Vou analisar o plano do doctor command e criar as tarefas adequadas. Primeiro, deixe-me ler o arquivo do plano e entender a estrutura do projeto.

content.content:
1→# Plan: O022 - `harness doctor` Command
     2→
     3→## Executive Summary
     4→
     5→Implementar o comando `harness doctor` que diagnostica a saúde de um projeto harness, com verificações básicas programáticas e opções avançadas de verificação via AI.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Research Summary
    10→
    11→### Fontes Pesquisadas
    12→
    13→1. **[Anthropic - Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)**
    14→   - Two-Agent Architecture (Initializer + Coding Agent)
    15→   - State files: `claude-progress.txt`, `features.json`, `init.sh`
    16→   - Session workflow and failure modes
    17→
    18→2. **[Anthropic - Claude Code Best Practices](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)**
    19→   - CLAUDE.md structure and usage
    20→   - Hooks configuration
    21→   - MCP integration patterns
    22→
    23→3. **[Anthropic - Building Agents with Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)**
    24→   - Context engineering principles
    25→   - Subagent patterns
    26→   - State management best practices
    27→
    28→4. **[Repomix - Output Formats](https://repomix.com/guide/output)**
    29→   - XML format (recommended for Claude)
    30→   - Markdown format for readability
    31→   - Token optimization strategies
    32→
    33→5. **[@clack/prompts](https://www.clack.cc/)**
    34→   - `multiselect()` for checkbox UI
    35→   - `group()` for composing prompts
    36→   - Beautiful terminal prompts out-of-box
    37→
    38→---
    39→
    40→## Architecture
    41→
    42→### Directory Structure
    43→
    44→```
    45→packages/harness-orchestrator/
    46→├── src/
    47→│   └── doctor/
    48→│       ├── index.ts                 # CLI command handler
    49→│       ├── BasicChecker.ts          # Programmatic checks (no AI)
    50→│       ├── AIChecker.ts             # AI-assisted verification
    51→│       └── KnowledgeManager.ts      # Manage .harness/kb/ files
    52→├── knowledge/                        # Source knowledge base
    53→│   ├── anthropic-harness-article.xml
    54→│   ├── claude-code-best-practices.xml
    55→│   ├── harness-schema.json
    56→│   └── README.md
    57→└── templates/
    58→    └── kb/                           # Templates copied to projects
    59→        ├── harness-guide.xml
    60→        └── best-practices.xml
    61→```
    62→
    63→### Knowledge Base Structure (`.harness/kb/`)
    64→
    65→Quando `harness init` ou `harness doctor --setup-kb` é executado, cria:
    66→
    67→```
    68→.harness/
    69→└── kb/                               # Knowledge Base for AI
    70→    ├── harness-guide.xml             # Core harness concepts
    71→    ├── best-practices.xml            # Recommendations
    72→    └── manifest.json                 # Version and metadata
    73→```
    74→
    75→**Formato XML (Repomix-style):**
    76→```xml
    77→<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    78→<knowledge-base version="1.0" updated="2025-12-16">
    79→  <document id="harness-guide">
    80→    <title>Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents</title>
    81→    <source>https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents</source>
    82→
    83→    <section id="core-problem">
    84→      <title>The Core Challenge</title>
    85→      <content>
    86→        Long-running AI agents must work in discrete sessions, and each new session
    87→        begins with no memory of what came before. This is like engineers working in
    88→        shifts where each new engineer has no memory of the previous shift.
    89→      </content>
    90→    </section>
    91→
    92→    <section id="two-agent-architecture">
    93→      <title>Two-Agent Architecture</title>
    94→      <content>
    95→        1. Initializer Agent (first session): Sets up environment, creates
    96→           claude-progress.txt, init.sh, and feature list.
    97→        2. Coding Agent (subsequent sessions): Works on ONE feature per session,
    98→           commits changes, updates progress file.
    99→      </content>
   100→    </section>
   101→
   102→    <!-- More sections... -->
   103→  </document>
   104→</knowledge-base>
   105→```
   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→## Feature Breakdown
   110→
   111→### O022-A: Basic Checker (Programmatic, No AI)
   112→
   113→**Verificações obrigatórias:**
   114→
   115→| Check | File/Dir | Description |
   116→|-------|----------|-------------|
   117→| `features.json` | Required | Feature definitions exist and valid JSON |
   118→| `claude-progress.txt` | Required | Session history exists |
   119→| `CLAUDE.md` | Required | AI instructions exist |
   120→| `init.sh` | Recommended | Dev environment script |
   121→| `.claude/settings.json` | Recommended | Hook configuration |
   122→| `.claude/hooks/` | Recommended | Hook scripts directory |
   123→| `.git/` | Required | Git repository initialized |
   124→| `package.json` | Optional | Node project detection |
   125→
   126→**Schema validation:**
   127→- `features.json` must have: `project`, `features[]`, each feature has `id`, `description`, `passes`, `blockedBy`
   128→- `CLAUDE.md` should mention: harness, progress, features
   129→
   130→**Output:**
   131→```
   132→🩺 Harness Doctor - Basic Check
   133→
   134→✓ features.json exists and valid
   135→✓ claude-progress.txt exists (4 sessions)
   136→✓ CLAUDE.md exists (contains harness instructions)
   137→✓ init.sh exists and executable
   138→✓ .claude/settings.json exists with hooks
   139→✓ .claude/hooks/ contains 4 hook scripts
   140→✓ .git/ repository initialized
   141→
   142→Summary: 7/7 checks passed
   143→Status: HEALTHY ✅
   144→```
   145→
   146→### O022-B: Knowledge Base Manager
   147→
   148→**Commands:**
   149→- `harness doctor --setup-kb` - Copy KB templates to `.harness/kb/`
   150→- KB files are versioned with `manifest.json`
   151→
   152→**Auto-detection:**
   153→- If `.harness/` doesn't exist, prompt user to create it
   154→- Explain purpose: "Knowledge Base for AI-assisted verification"
   155→
   156→### O022-C: Interactive Mode with @clack/prompts
   157→
   158→**Dependency:** Add `@clack/prompts` to package.json
   159→
   160→**UI Flow:**
   161→```typescript
   162→import * as p from '@clack/prompts';
   163→
   164→p.intro('🩺 Harness Doctor');
   165→
   166→const options = await p.multiselect({
   167→  message: 'Select verification options:',
   168→  options: [
   169→    { value: 'ai-structure', label: 'AI Structure Verification', hint: 'AI validates harness setup' },
   170→    { value: 'ai-health', label: 'AI Health Check', hint: 'AI reviews task quality & Anthropic best practices' },
   171→  ],
   172→  required: false,
   173→});
   174→
   175→// Basic checks always run first
   176→const basicResults = await runBasicChecks();
   177→
   178→// Then run selected AI checks
   179→if (options.includes('ai-structure')) {
   180→  await runAIStructureCheck();
   181→}
   182→if (options.includes('ai-health')) {
   183→  await runAIHealthCheck();
   184→}
   185→
   186→p.outro('Doctor complete!');
   187→```
   188→
   189→### O022-D: AI Structure Verification
   190→
   191→**What it checks:**
   192→1. Does the harness follow Anthropic's Two-Agent Architecture?
   193→2. Is `claude-progress.txt` properly formatted with sessions?
   194→3. Are hooks correctly configured for the protocol?
   195→4. Is `CLAUDE.md` comprehensive enough?
   196→
   197→**How it works:**
   198→1. Read `.harness/kb/` files (local knowledge)
   199→2. If KB missing/outdated, offer to update via web fetch
   200→3. Spawn Claude with KB context + current harness files
   201→4. Claude validates structure against known patterns
   202→
   203→**Prompt template:**
   204→```
   205→You are a harness structure validator. Using the knowledge base provided,
   206→verify this harness follows Anthropic's recommended patterns.
   207→
   208→<knowledge-base>
   209→{content from .harness/kb/}
   210→</knowledge-base>
   211→
   212→<harness-files>
   213→<file path="features.json">{content}</file>
   214→<file path="claude-progress.txt">{content}</file>
   215→<file path="CLAUDE.md">{content}</file>
   216→</harness-files>
   217→
   218→Validate:
   219→1. Two-Agent Architecture compliance
   220→2. Session workflow correctness
   221→3. Hook configuration
   222→4. CLAUDE.md completeness
   223→
   224→Output JSON:
   225→{
   226→  "valid": boolean,
   227→  "issues": [{ "severity": "error|warning|info", "message": string, "fix": string }],
   228→  "score": number (0-100)
   229→}
   230→```
   231→
   232→### O022-E: AI Health Check
   233→
   234→**What it checks:**
   235→1. Task quality (descriptions, steps, dependencies)
   236→2. Progress log quality (sessions documented properly)
   237→3. Best practices adherence
   238→4. Potential issues/blockers
   239→
   240→**Analysis areas:**
   241→- Feature granularity (not too big, not too small)
   242→- Dependency cycles or dead-ends
   243→- Missing documentation
   244→- Stale progress (no recent sessions)
   245→- Orphaned features (no dependencies or dependents)
   246→
   247→**Output:**
   248→```
   249→🔬 AI Health Analysis
   250→
   251→📊 Task Quality: 85/100
   252→   ✓ Feature descriptions are clear
   253→   ⚠️ 3 features have vague steps
   254→   ⚠️ F012 has no steps defined
   255→
   256→📈 Progress Quality: 92/100
   257→   ✓ Sessions well documented
   258→   ✓ Regular updates
   259→
   260→🏗️ Architecture: 78/100
   261→   ⚠️ Deep dependency chain (8 levels) may slow execution
   262→   ✓ No cycles detected
   263→
   264→💡 Recommendations:
   265→   1. Add steps to F012
   266→   2. Consider breaking F007 into smaller features
   267→   3. Update progress.txt - last session was 5 days ago
   268→```
   269→
   270→---
   271→
   272→## Implementation Plan
   273→
   274→### Step 1: Add Dependencies
   275→
   276→```bash
   277→cd packages/harness-orchestrator
   278→bun add @clack/prompts
   279→```
   280→
   281→### Step 2: Create Knowledge Base Templates
   282→
   283→Create `packages/harness-orchestrator/knowledge/`:
   284→- Fetch and process Anthropic articles
   285→- Convert to optimized XML format
   286→- Create schema file for validation
   287→
   288→### Step 3: Implement BasicChecker
   289→
   290→```typescript
   291→// src/doctor/BasicChecker.ts
   292→export interface CheckResult {
   293→  name: string;
   294→  passed: boolean;
   295→  message: string;
   296→  severity: 'error' | 'warning' | 'info';
   297→  fix?: string;
   298→}
   299→
   300→export class BasicChecker {
   301→  constructor(private projectPath: string) {}
   302→
   303→  async runAllChecks(): Promise<CheckResult[]> {
   304→    return [
   305→      await this.checkFeaturesJson(),
   306→      await this.checkProgressFile(),
   307→      await this.checkClaudeMd(),
   308→      await this.checkInitSh(),
   309→      await this.checkHooksDir(),
   310→      await this.checkGitRepo(),
   311→      await this.checkHarnessKb(),
   312→    ];
   313→  }
   314→
   315→  private async checkFeaturesJson(): Promise<CheckResult> { ... }
   316→  private async checkHarnessKb(): Promise<CheckResult> { ... }
   317→  // etc.
   318→}
   319→```
   320→
   321→### Step 4: Implement KnowledgeManager
   322→
   323→```typescript
   324→// src/doctor/KnowledgeManager.ts
   325→export class KnowledgeManager {
   326→  constructor(private projectPath: string) {}
   327→
   328→  async exists(): Promise<boolean> {
   329→    return existsSync(join(this.projectPath, '.harness', 'kb'));
   330→  }
   331→
   332→  async setup(): Promise<void> {
   333→    // Copy templates from package to project
   334→  }
   335→
   336→  async getVersion(): Promise<string> {
   337→    // Read manifest.json version
   338→  }
   339→
   340→  async needsUpdate(): Promise<boolean> {
   341→    // Compare with bundled version
   342→  }
   343→
   344→  async readForAI(): Promise<string> {
   345→    // Return concatenated XML content for AI context
   346→  }
   347→}
   348→```
   349→
   350→### Step 5: Implement AIChecker
   351→
   352→```typescript
   353→// src/doctor/AIChecker.ts
   354→export class AIChecker {
   355→  constructor(
   356→    private projectPath: string,
   357→    private knowledgeManager: KnowledgeManager
   358→  ) {}
   359→
   360→  async checkStructure(): Promise<AICheckResult> {
   361→    const kb = await this.knowledgeManager.readForAI();
   362→    const files = await this.readHarnessFiles();
   363→
   364→    // Spawn Claude with verification prompt
   365→    // Parse JSON response
   366→  }
   367→
   368→  async checkHealth(): Promise<AICheckResult> {
   369→    // Similar, but different prompt for health analysis
   370→  }
   371→}
   372→```
   373→
   374→### Step 6: Implement CLI Command
   375→
   376→```typescript
   377→// src/doctor/index.ts (or add to cli/index.ts)
   378→program
   379→  .command('doctor [project-path]')
   380→  .description('Diagnose harness health with optional AI verification')
   381→  .option('--setup-kb', 'Setup or update .harness/kb/ knowledge base')
   382→  .option('--no-interactive', 'Run basic checks only, no prompts')
   383→  .option('--json', 'Output results as JSON')
   384→  .action(async (projectPath, options) => {
   385→    // Implementation
   386→  });
   387→```
   388→
   389→---
   390→
   391→## Features.json Entry
   392→
   393→```json
   394→{
   395→  "id": "O022",
   396→  "category": "doctor",
   397→  "description": "Implement 'harness doctor' diagnostic command",
   398→  "steps": [
   399→    "Add @clack/prompts dependency",
   400→    "Create knowledge base templates (XML format)",
   401→    "Implement BasicChecker (programmatic validation)",
   402→    "Implement KnowledgeManager (.harness/kb/ management)",
   403→    "Implement AIChecker (structure + health verification)",
   404→    "Add 'harness doctor' CLI command with multiselect UI",
   405→    "Test on harness-dashboard project",
   406→    "Update harness init to mention doctor command"
   407→  ],
   408→  "passes": false,
   409→  "blockedBy": "O020",
   410→  "notes": "Uses @clack/prompts for beautiful checkbox UI. Knowledge base in .harness/kb/ per project."
   411→}
   412→```
   413→
   414→---
   415→
   416→## Test Scenarios
   417→
   418→### Scenario 1: Healthy Project
   419→```bash
   420→$ harness doctor

file.content:
# Plan: O022 - `harness doctor` Command

## Executive Summary

Implementar o comando `harness doctor` que diagnostica a saúde de um projeto harness, com verificações básicas programáticas e opções avançadas de verificação via AI.

---

## Research Summary

### Fontes Pesquisadas

1. **[Anthropic - Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)**
   - Two-Agent Architecture (Initializer + Coding Agent)
   - State files: `claude-progress.txt`, `features.json`, `init.sh`
   - Session workflow and failure modes

2. **[Anthropic - Claude Code Best Practices](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices)**
   - CLAUDE.md structure and usage
   - Hooks configuration
   - MCP integration patterns

3. **[Anthropic - Building Agents with Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)**
   - Context engineering principles
   - Subagent patterns
   - State management best practices

4. **[Repomix - Output Formats](https://repomix.com/guide/output)**
   - XML format (recommended for Claude)
   - Markdown format for readability
   - Token optimization strategies

5. **[@clack/prompts](https://www.clack.cc/)**
   - `multiselect()` for checkbox UI
   - `group()` for composing prompts
   - Beautiful terminal prompts out-of-box

---

## Architecture

### Directory Structure

```
packages/harness-orchestrator/
├── src/
│   └── doctor/
│       ├── index.ts                 # CLI command handler
│       ├── BasicChecker.ts          # Programmatic checks (no AI)
│       ├── AIChecker.ts             # AI-assisted verification
│       └── KnowledgeManager.ts      # Manage .harness/kb/ files
├── knowledge/                        # Source knowledge base
│   ├── anthropic-harness-article.xml
│   ├── claude-code-best-practices.xml
│   ├── harness-schema.json
│   └── README.md
└── templates/
    └── kb/                           # Templates copied to projects
        ├── harness-guide.xml
        └── best-practices.xml
```

### Knowledge Base Structure (`.harness/kb/`)

Quando `harness init` ou `harness doctor --setup-kb` é executado, cria:

```
.harness/
└── kb/                               # Knowledge Base for AI
    ├── harness-guide.xml             # Core harness concepts
    ├── best-practices.xml            # Recommendations
    └── manifest.json                 # Version and metadata
```

**Formato XML (Repomix-style):**
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<knowledge-base version="1.0" updated="2025-12-16">
  <document id="harness-guide">
    <title>Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents</title>
    <source>https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents</source>

    <section id="core-problem">
      <title>The Core Challenge</title>
      <content>
        Long-running AI agents must work in discrete sessions, and each new session
        begins with no memory of what came before. This is like engineers working in
        shifts where each new engineer has no memory of the previous shift.
      </content>
    </section>

    <section id="two-agent-architecture">
      <title>Two-Agent Architecture</title>
      <content>
        1. Initializer Agent (first session): Sets up environment, creates
           claude-progress.txt, init.sh, and feature list.
        2. Coding Agent (subsequent sessions): Works on ONE feature per session,
           commits changes, updates progress file.
      </content>
    </section>

    <!-- More sections... -->
  </document>
</knowledge-base>
```

---

## Feature Breakdown

### O022-A: Basic Checker (Programmatic, No AI)

**Verificações obrigatórias:**

| Check | File/Dir | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `features.json` | Required | Feature definitions exist and valid JSON |
| `claude-progress.txt` | Required | Session history exists |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Required | AI instructions exist |
| `init.sh` | Recommended | Dev environment script |
| `.claude/settings.json` | Recommended | Hook configuration |
| `.claude/hooks/` | Recommended | Hook scripts directory |
| `.git/` | Required | Git repository initialized |
| `package.json` | Optional | Node project detection |

**Schema validation:**
- `features.json` must have: `project`, `features[]`, each feature has `id`, `description`, `passes`, `blockedBy`
- `CLAUDE.md` should mention: harness, progress, features

**Output:**
```
🩺 Harness Doctor - Basic Check

✓ features.json exists and valid
✓ claude-progress.txt exists (4 sessions)
✓ CLAUDE.md exists (contains harness instructions)
✓ init.sh exists and executable
✓ .claude/settings.json exists with hooks
✓ .claude/hooks/ contains 4 hook scripts
✓ .git/ repository initialized

Summary: 7/7 checks passed
Status: HEALTHY ✅
```

### O022-B: Knowledge Base Manager

**Commands:**
- `harness doctor --setup-kb` - Copy KB templates to `.harness/kb/`
- KB files are versioned with `manifest.json`

**Auto-detection:**
- If `.harness/` doesn't exist, prompt user to create it
- Explain purpose: "Knowledge Base for AI-assisted verification"

### O022-C: Interactive Mode with @clack/prompts

**Dependency:** Add `@clack/prompts` to package.json

**UI Flow:**
```typescript
import * as p from '@clack/prompts';

p.intro('🩺 Harness Doctor');

const options = await p.multiselect({
  message: 'Select verification options:',
  options: [
    { value: 'ai-structure', label: 'AI Structure Verification', hint: 'AI validates harness setup' },
    { value: 'ai-health', label: 'AI Health Check', hint: 'AI reviews task quality & Anthropic best practices' },
  ],
  required: false,
});

// Basic checks always run first
const basicResults = await runBasicChecks();

// Then run selected AI checks
if (options.includes('ai-structure')) {
  await runAIStructureCheck();
}
if (options.includes('ai-health')) {
  await runAIHealthCheck();
}

p.outro('Doctor complete!');
```

### O022-D: AI Structure Verification

**What it checks:**
1. Does the harness follow Anthropic's Two-Agent Architecture?
2. Is `claude-progress.txt` properly formatted with sessions?
3. Are hooks correctly configured for the protocol?
4. Is `CLAUDE.md` comprehensive enough?

**How it works:**
1. Read `.harness/kb/` files (local knowledge)
2. If KB missing/outdated, offer to update via web fetch
3. Spawn Claude with KB context + current harness files
4. Claude validates structure against known patterns

**Prompt template:**
```
You are a harness structure validator. Using the knowledge base provided,
verify this harness follows Anthropic's recommended patterns.

<knowledge-base>
{content from .harness/kb/}
</knowledge-base>

<harness-files>
<file path="features.json">{content}</file>
<file path="claude-progress.txt">{content}</file>
<file path="CLAUDE.md">{content}</file>
</harness-files>

Validate:
1. Two-Agent Architecture compliance
2. Session workflow correctness
3. Hook configuration
4. CLAUDE.md completeness

Output JSON:
{
  "valid": boolean,
  "issues": [{ "severity": "error|warning|info", "message": string, "fix": string }],
  "score": number (0-100)
}
```

### O022-E: AI Health Check

**What it checks:**
1. Task quality (descriptions, steps, dependencies)
2. Progress log quality (sessions documented properly)
3. Best practices adherence
4. Potential issues/blockers

**Analysis areas:**
- Feature granularity (not too big, not too small)
- Dependency cycles or dead-ends
- Missing documentation
- Stale progress (no recent sessions)
- Orphaned features (no dependencies or dependents)

**Output:**
```
🔬 AI Health Analysis

📊 Task Quality: 85/100
   ✓ Feature descriptions are clear
   ⚠️ 3 features have vague steps
   ⚠️ F012 has no steps defined

📈 Progress Quality: 92/100
   ✓ Sessions well documented
   ✓ Regular updates

🏗️ Architecture: 78/100
   ⚠️ Deep dependency chain (8 levels) may slow execution
   ✓ No cycles detected

💡 Recommendations:
   1. Add steps to F012
   2. Consider breaking F007 into smaller features
   3. Update progress.txt - last session was 5 days ago
```

---

bun add @clack/prompts

{
  "project": "MMS Harness Orchestrator",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "AI Project Manager that orchestrates multiple Claude agents to complete harness tasks in parallel",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16T03",
  "goals": [
    "Analyze task dependencies and identify parallelizable work",
    "Dispatch multiple Claude agents simultaneously",
    "Optimize for quality, speed, and cost",
    "Track progress and handle failures gracefully",
    "Isolate from main project to avoid conflicts"
  ],
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "O001",
      "category": "setup",
      "description": "Package initialization with dependencies",
      "steps": [
        "Create package.json with type: module",
        "Add dependencies: commander, chalk, ora (spinners)",
        "Setup TypeScript configuration",
        "Create src/ directory structure"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Isolated package to avoid conflicts with main project"
    },
    {
      "id": "O002",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Dependency graph analyzer",
      "steps": [
        "Parse features.json into internal model",
        "Build directed acyclic graph (DAG) from blockedBy relations",
        "Implement topological sort for execution order",
        "Identify independent tasks (parallelizable)",
        "Calculate critical path"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O001",
      "notes": "Core algorithm for understanding task relationships"
    },
    {
      "id": "O003",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Task scheduler with parallel execution",
      "steps": [
        "Implement ready queue (tasks with all deps satisfied)",
        "Track in-progress tasks",
        "Limit concurrent agents (configurable)",
        "Handle task completion and unlock dependents",
        "Implement priority scoring (critical path first)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O002",
      "notes": "Scheduler determines what runs when"
    },
    {
      "id": "O004",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Agent pool manager",
      "steps": [
        "Spawn Claude processes with isolated contexts",
        "Manage agent lifecycle (start, monitor, stop)",
        "Capture stdout/stderr for logging",
        "Handle agent crashes gracefully",
        "Implement agent timeout protection"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O001",
      "notes": "Manages multiple Claude instances"
    },
    {
      "id": "O005",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "State manager with file locking",
      "steps": [
        "Atomic updates to features.json",
        "File locking to prevent race conditions",
        "State snapshots for recovery",
        "Progress persistence across restarts",
        "Conflict resolution for concurrent updates"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O001",
      "notes": "Critical for multi-agent coordination"
    },
    {
      "id": "O006",
      "category": "orchestrator",
      "description": "Main orchestrator loop",
      "steps": [
        "Initialize dependency graph",
        "Main loop: schedule -> dispatch -> wait -> update",
        "Handle completion events",
        "Detect and handle deadlocks",
        "Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": ["O002", "O003", "O004", "O005"],
      "notes": "Central coordination logic"
    },
    {
      "id": "O007",
      "category": "intelligence",
      "description": "AI Project Manager agent",
      "steps": [
        "Analyze task complexity for model selection",
        "Generate optimized prompts per task type",
        "Review completed work quality",
        "Suggest task reordering for efficiency",
        "Provide progress summaries"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O006",
      "notes": "The 'brain' that makes smart decisions"
    },
    {
      "id": "O008",
      "category": "cli",
      "description": "CLI interface with commander.js",
      "steps": [
        "orchestrate command - run full orchestration",
        "analyze command - show dependency graph",
        "status command - show current progress",
        "Options: --max-agents, --model, --dry-run",
        "Colored output with chalk"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O006",
      "notes": "User-friendly command line interface"
    },
    {
      "id": "O009",
      "category": "monitoring",
      "description": "Real-time TERMINAL dashboard (NOT web UI)",
      "steps": [
        "Terminal UI using chalk/ora (command line only)",
        "Progress bars per feature rendered in terminal",
        "Cost tracking displayed in terminal output",
        "Time estimates shown in terminal",
        "Log streaming from agents to stdout"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O008",
      "notes": "CLARIFICATION: This is a TERMINAL-BASED dashboard (src/dashboard/Dashboard.ts) using chalk for colors. NOT a web dashboard with Astro/React. It renders progress directly in the command line when running 'harness orchestrate --dashboard' or 'harness dashboard'."
    },
    {
      "id": "O010",
      "category": "quality",
      "description": "Verification and rollback system",
      "steps": [
        "Git branch per feature for isolation",
        "Automated verification of passes criteria",
        "Rollback failed features",
        "Merge successful features to main",
        "Conflict resolution strategy"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O006",
      "notes": "GitManager + Verifier classes with CLI commands (verify, rollback, branches)"
    },
    {
      "id": "O011",
      "category": "bugfix",
      "description": "Commit pending code review fixes",
      "steps": [
        "Commit ClaudeAgent.ts fix (JSON.stringify double quotes)",
        "Commit cli/index.ts fix (async/await in verify and rollback)",
        "Commit Verifier.ts fix (resolveConflicts dead code)",
        "Commit Dashboard.ts fix (maxAgents hardcoded)",
        "Commit README.md (new documentation)",
        "Commit progress.txt updates"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O010",
      "notes": "Committed 9d3d27b with all fixes + README.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "O012",
      "category": "cli",
      "description": "Rename CLI to 'harness' with alias 'h'",
      "steps": [
        "Update package.json bin to 'harness' and 'h'",
        "Update README.md with new command names",
        "Re-link globally with bun link",
        "Test both 'harness' and 'h' commands work"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O011",
      "notes": "User requested simpler command name"
    },
    {
      "id": "O013",
      "category": "cli",
      "description": "Make features.json path optional with default",
      "steps": [
        "Change all commands from <features-path> to [features-path]",
        "Default to 'features.json' in current directory",
        "Update help text to reflect optional parameter",
        "Test all commands work without path argument"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O011",
      "notes": "Reduce typing for common case"
    },
    {
      "id": "O014",
      "category": "research",
      "description": "Study Anthropic Harness protocol via web search",
      "steps": [
        "WebSearch 'Anthropic harness agents 2025'",
        "Read official Anthropic documentation on harnesses",
        "Understand the complete harness protocol",
        "Document findings in NOTES.md",
        "Compare with current root implementation"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O011",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Documented in NOTES.md. Key learnings: Two-agent architecture (Initializer + Coding), claude-progress.txt for state, ONE feature per session, visual testing mandatory, Agent SDK with hooks/MCP/sessions"
    },
    {
      "id": "O015",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement 'harness init' command",
      "steps": [
        "Create init command that generates harness structure",
        "Generate features.json template",
        "Generate progress.txt / claude-progress.txt template",
        "Generate CLAUDE.md template",
        "Generate .claude/settings.json",
        "Generate .claude/hooks/ with standard hooks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O014",
      "notes": "Bootstrap new harness projects with full structure"
    },
    {
      "id": "O016",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement hooks system matching root .claude/hooks/",
      "steps": [
        "Study root hooks: session-start.sh, end-of-turn-check.sh, protect-package-json.sh, after-write.sh, task-type-detector.py",
        "Implement hook templates in CLI",
        "Ensure hooks enforce harness protocol",
        "Ensure hooks prevent commits without proper workflow",
        "Ensure hooks trigger visual testing (MCP Playwright)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O014",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Created HookTemplates.ts with all 4 hooks (session-start, end-of-turn-check, protect-package-json, after-write). Integrated into init command."
    },
    {
      "id": "O017",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement progress.txt / claude-progress.txt management",
      "steps": [
        "Add 'harness session' command to create new session entry",
        "Add 'harness log' command to append to progress",
        "Parse and display progress history",
        "Integrate with status command"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O015",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Added session, log, and progress commands. Session auto-detects session number and includes feature status."
    },
    {
      "id": "O018",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement visual testing integration (MCP Playwright)",
      "steps": [
        "Study how root harness uses MCP Playwright for visual tests",
        "Add visual test triggers to hooks",
        "Ensure features cannot be marked complete without visual verification",
        "Document visual testing protocol in generated CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O016",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Created src/testing/VisualTesting.ts with: generateVisualTestCommands(), generateQAChecklist(), requiresVisualTesting(), getMcpConfig(). Added CLI commands: 'harness test' (generate Playwright MCP commands), 'harness test --checklist' (QA checklist), 'harness test --setup' (generate .mcp.json)."
    },
    {
      "id": "O019",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement commit workflow enforcement",
      "steps": [
        "Study how root hooks handle git commits",
        "Ensure commits only happen after proper workflow",
        "Prevent commits without feature completion",
        "Add commit message templates following harness protocol"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O016",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Created src/workflow/CommitWorkflow.ts with: validateCommit(), checkFeatureReadyForCommit(), generateGitCommitCommand(), getStagedFiles(). Added CLI commands: 'harness commit -f <id>' (validate and prepare commit), 'harness pre-commit' (for git hooks). Validates: passes:true in features.json, progress updated, no sensitive files, staged changes."
    },
    {
      "id": "O020",
      "category": "testing",
      "description": "E2E test: Validate harness-orchestrator CLI using harness-dashboard as test subject",
      "steps": [
        "Use packages/harness-dashboard as a REAL PROJECT to test our CLI",
        "Run 'harness status' on harness-dashboard and verify output",
        "Run 'harness analyze' on harness-dashboard and verify dependency graph",
        "Run 'harness verify' on harness-dashboard and verify checks work",
        "Test hooks execute properly when running commands",
        "Document any CLI bugs or issues found during testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": ["O018", "O019"],
      "notes": "CLARIFICATION: This is NOT about building harness-dashboard. harness-dashboard is a SEPARATE project (an Astro/React web dashboard) that happens to use the harness pattern. We use it as a TEST SUBJECT to validate that our harness-orchestrator CLI works correctly on real projects. TESTED: status (32 features displayed), analyze (critical path D001→D025, no cycles), test --checklist (auto-selected D001), verify (git status shown). All commands work correctly."
    },
    {
      "id": "O021",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement 'harness continue' command per Anthropic protocol",
      "steps": [
        "Detect if first run (no progress.txt) → spawn Initializer Agent",
        "If progress.txt exists → spawn Coding Agent",
        "Coding Agent: read state, pick highest-priority incomplete feature",
        "Work on ONE feature per invocation (prevents context overflow)",
        "Commit to git with descriptive messages",
        "Update claude-progress.txt before session end",
        "Integrate with Playwright MCP for visual testing",
        "Use --no-session-persistence for fresh context each run"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": ["O015", "O016"],
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Two-Agent Architecture implemented. Initializer creates progress.txt and runs init.sh. Coding Agent uses DependencyGraph + CriticalPath to pick next feature and spawns Claude."
    }
  ],
  "architecture": {
    "overview": "Multi-agent orchestration system using Claude Code CLI",
    "components": {
      "DependencyGraph": "Analyzes blockedBy relations, finds parallel tasks",
      "TaskScheduler": "Manages ready queue, tracks in-progress work",
      "AgentPool": "Spawns and manages Claude processes",
      "StateManager": "Atomic state updates with file locking",
      "Orchestrator": "Main loop coordinating all components",
      "AIManager": "Makes intelligent decisions about task execution"
    },
    "dataFlow": [
      "1. Load features.json -> Build DAG",
      "2. Identify ready tasks (no pending deps)",
      "3. Score and prioritize (critical path)",
      "4. Dispatch to available agents",
      "5. Monitor completion, update state",
      "6. Unlock dependent tasks, repeat"
    ]
  },
  "considerations": {
    "isolation": "Run in packages/harness-orchestrator to avoid conflicts",
    "atomicity": "Use file locking for features.json updates",
    "recovery": "Snapshot state before each batch for rollback",
    "cost": "Track API usage, use cheaper models for simple tasks",
    "quality": "Verify each feature before marking complete"
  }
}

1→# Harness Orchestrator - Progress Log
     2→
     3→## Project Goal
     4→Create an AI Project Manager that orchestrates multiple Claude agents to complete harness tasks in parallel, optimizing for quality, speed, and cost.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Session 1 - 2025-12-16 - Harness Setup
     9→
    10→### Context
    11→User requested automation of the MMS harness with:
    12→- AI acting as project manager
    13→- Parallel task execution
    14→- Dependency analysis
    15→- Quality assurance
    16→
    17→### Completed ✅
    18→1. Created basic `scripts/harness-runner.js` - sequential runner (committed: `a571f97`)
    19→2. Created `packages/harness-orchestrator/` - isolated package (committed: `0aab9bf`)
    20→3. Defined features.json with 10 features (O001-O010)
    21→4. Created CLAUDE.md with architecture and instructions
    22→5. Created NOTES.md with technical learnings pattern
    23→
    24→### Architecture Decisions
    25→
    26→#### Why Isolated Package?
    27→- Avoid dependency conflicts with main project
    28→- Can install own deps (commander, chalk, ora)
    29→- Separate harness tracking
    30→- Won't interfere with parallel agents on main project
    31→
    32→#### Core Components Planned
    33→```
    34→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    35→│                    ORCHESTRATOR                              │
    36→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    37→│                                                              │
    38→│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
    39→│  │  Dependency  │───▶│    Task      │───▶│    Agent     │  │
    40→│  │    Graph     │    │  Scheduler   │    │    Pool      │  │
    41→│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
    42→│         │                   │                   │           │
    43→│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
    44→│                             ▼                               │
    45→│                    ┌──────────────┐                         │
    46→│                    │    State     │                         │
    47→│                    │   Manager    │                         │
    48→│                    └──────────────┘                         │
    49→│                             │                               │
    50→│                             ▼                               │
    51→│                    ┌──────────────┐                         │
    52→│                    │     AI       │                         │
    53→│                    │   Manager    │                         │
    54→│                    └──────────────┘                         │
    55→│                                                              │
    56→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    57→```
    58→
    59→#### Parallelization Strategy
    60→1. Build DAG from features.json blockedBy
    61→2. Find all nodes with no pending dependencies (ready queue)
    62→3. Execute ready tasks in parallel (up to max-agents limit)
    63→4. On completion: update state, unlock dependents, repeat
    64→
    65→#### Critical Path Optimization
    66→- Calculate longest path through DAG
    67→- Prioritize tasks on critical path
    68→- Reduces total completion time
    69→
    70→### Current State
    71→| Feature | Status | Notes |
    72→|---------|--------|-------|
    73→| O001 | ❌ Pending | Package initialization |
    74→| O002 | ❌ Pending | Dependency graph (blocked by O001) |
    75→| O003 | ❌ Pending | Task scheduler (blocked by O002) |
    76→| O004 | ❌ Pending | Agent pool (blocked by O001) |
    77→| O005 | ❌ Pending | State manager (blocked by O001) |
    78→| O006 | ❌ Pending | Main orchestrator (blocked by O002-O005) |
    79→| O007 | ❌ Pending | AI manager (blocked by O006) |
    80→| O008 | ❌ Pending | CLI interface (blocked by O006) |
    81→| O009 | ❌ Pending | Dashboard (blocked by O008) |
    82→| O010 | ❌ Pending | Verification system (blocked by O006) |
    83→
    84→### Parallelization Analysis
    85→After O001 completes, these can run in parallel:
    86→- O002 (Dependency graph)
    87→- O004 (Agent pool)
    88→- O005 (State manager)
    89→
    90→This is a 3x speedup opportunity!
    91→
    92→### Key Files
    93→| File | Purpose |
    94→|------|---------|
    95→| `features.json` | Feature definitions and status |
    96→| `progress.txt` | This session log |
    97→| `CLAUDE.md` | AI instructions and architecture |
    98→| `NOTES.md` | Technical learnings and gotchas |
    99→
   100→### Learnings from Main Project
   101→Applied patterns from MMS main project:
   102→1. ✅ Structured progress log with session markers
   103→2. ✅ Technical notes file (NOTES.md) for gotchas

   114→## Quick Start for New Session
   115→
   116→```bash
   117→# 1. Navigate to package
   118→cd packages/harness-orchestrator
   119→
   120→# 2. Read state (do this every time!)
   121→cat progress.txt | head -100

   123→
   124→# 3. Check NOTES.md for gotchas

   127→# 4. Work on next feature

   130→# 5. Update status

   134→
   135→# 6. Commit ONLY this package
   136→git add packages/harness-orchestrator/

   138→```
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→## Session 2 - 2025-12-16 - O001 Implementation
   143→

   145→

   148→1. Created `package.json` with:

   154→2. Created `tsconfig.json` with:

   160→3. Created full `src/` directory structure:
   161→   ```
   162→   src/
   163→   ├── types.ts           # Core type definitions
   164→   ├── index.ts           # Main exports
   165→   ├── graph/
   166→   │   ├── DependencyGraph.ts   # DAG builder
   167→   │   └── CriticalPath.ts      # Priority calculator
   168→   ├── scheduler/
   169→   │   ├── TaskScheduler.ts     # Ready queue management
   170→   │   └── PriorityQueue.ts     # Min-heap implementation
   171→   ├── agents/

   173→   │   └── ClaudeAgent.ts       # Single agent wrapper
   174→   ├── state/

   179→   └── cli/

   181→   ```
   182→
   183→4. Verified:

   185→   - Build succeeds (`bun run build`)
   186→   - CLI works (`node dist/cli/index.js --help`)
   187→   - `analyze` command shows dependency graph

   189→
   190→### Key Decisions

   192→1. **Went beyond minimum**: Created full src/ structure with placeholder implementations
   193→   - Reason: CLI commands need imports to work

   196→2. **Used proper-lockfile**: Industry-standard file locking
   197→   - Prevents race conditions in multi-agent updates
   198→
   199→3. **Implemented Priority Queue**: Min-heap for task scheduling
   200→   - Critical path tasks get higher priority

   564→## Session 7 - 2025-12-16 - Anthropic Protocol Research + CLI Improvements
   565→
   566→### Context
   567→User requested to prioritize researching the official Anthropic Harness protocol before implementing any features. This was critical to ensure correct implementation.
   568→
   569→### Completed ✅
   570→
   571→**O011: Commit pending code review fixes**
   572→- Committed all 5 bug fixes from Session 5
   573→- Added README.md documentation
   574→- Commit: 9d3d27b
   575→
   576→**O014: Research Anthropic Harness protocol** ⭐ CRITICAL
   577→- Read official article: https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents
   578→- Read Claude Agent SDK docs: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview
   579→- Documented all findings in NOTES.md
   580→
   581→**Key Protocol Learnings:**
   582→1. **Two-Agent Architecture**:
   583→   - Initializer Agent: Runs ONCE on first session, creates claude-progress.txt, init.sh, features.json
   584→   - Coding Agent: Runs in ALL subsequent sessions, works on ONE feature per invocation
   585→
   586→2. **Session Workflow**:
   587→   - Read git logs + progress files
   588→   - Run init.sh to start dev server
   589→   - Pick highest-priority incomplete feature
   590→   - Implement ONE feature
   591→   - Commit with descriptive messages
   592→   - Update claude-progress.txt
   593→
   594→3. **Visual Testing is MANDATORY**:
   595→   - Claude cannot identify non-working features without UI testing
   596→   - Must use Playwright MCP for browser automation
   597→
   598→4. **`harness continue` Command**:
   599→   - Core command that implements the protocol
   600→   - Detects first run (no progress.txt) → spawn Initializer
   601→   - Otherwise → spawn Coding Agent
   602→   - Each invocation = one feature completed
   603→
   604→**O012: Rename CLI to 'harness' with alias 'h'**
   605→- Updated package.json bin entries
   606→- Updated CLI program name and alias
   607→- Updated README.md with new command names
   608→- CLI now shows: `Usage: harness|h [options] [command]`
   609→

   611→- All commands now use `[features-path]` (optional)
   612→- Default: `features.json` in current directory

# Harness Orchestrator - Progress Log

## Project Goal
Create an AI Project Manager that orchestrates multiple Claude agents to complete harness tasks in parallel, optimizing for quality, speed, and cost.

---

## Session 1 - 2025-12-16 - Harness Setup

### Context
User requested automation of the MMS harness with:
- AI acting as project manager
- Parallel task execution
- Dependency analysis
- Quality assurance

### Completed ✅
1. Created basic `scripts/harness-runner.js` - sequential runner (committed: `a571f97`)
2. Created `packages/harness-orchestrator/` - isolated package (committed: `0aab9bf`)
3. Defined features.json with 10 features (O001-O010)
4. Created CLAUDE.md with architecture and instructions
5. Created NOTES.md with technical learnings pattern

### Architecture Decisions

#### Why Isolated Package?
- Avoid dependency conflicts with main project
- Can install own deps (commander, chalk, ora)
- Separate harness tracking
- Won't interfere with parallel agents on main project

#### Core Components Planned
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    ORCHESTRATOR                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐  │
│  │  Dependency  │───▶│    Task      │───▶│    Agent     │  │
│  │    Graph     │    │  Scheduler   │    │    Pool      │  │
│  └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘  │
│         │                   │                   │           │
│         └───────────────────┼───────────────────┘           │
│                             ▼                               │
│                    ┌──────────────┐                         │
│                    │    State     │                         │
│                    │   Manager    │                         │
│                    └──────────────┘                         │
│                             │                               │
│                             ▼                               │
│                    ┌──────────────┐                         │
│                    │     AI       │                         │
│                    │   Manager    │                         │
│                    └──────────────┘                         │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Parallelization Strategy
1. Build DAG from features.json blockedBy
2. Find all nodes with no pending dependencies (ready queue)
3. Execute ready tasks in parallel (up to max-agents limit)
4. On completion: update state, unlock dependents, repeat

#### Critical Path Optimization
- Calculate longest path through DAG
- Prioritize tasks on critical path
- Reduces total completion time

### Current State
| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| O001 | ❌ Pending | Package initialization |
| O002 | ❌ Pending | Dependency graph (blocked by O001) |
| O003 | ❌ Pending | Task scheduler (blocked by O002) |
| O004 | ❌ Pending | Agent pool (blocked by O001) |
| O005 | ❌ Pending | State manager (blocked by O001) |
| O006 | ❌ Pending | Main orchestrator (blocked by O002-O005) |
| O007 | ❌ Pending | AI manager (blocked by O006) |
| O008 | ❌ Pending | CLI interface (blocked by O006) |
| O009 | ❌ Pending | Dashboard (blocked by O008) |
| O010 | ❌ Pending | Verification system (blocked by O006) |

### Parallelization Analysis
After O001 completes, these can run in parallel:
- O002 (Dependency graph)
- O004 (Agent pool)
- O005 (State manager)

This is a 3x speedup opportunity!

### Key Files
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `features.json` | Feature definitions and status |
| `progress.txt` | This session log |
| `CLAUDE.md` | AI instructions and architecture |
| `NOTES.md` | Technical learnings and gotchas |

### Learnings from Main Project
Applied patterns from MMS main project:
1. ✅ Structured progress log with session markers
2. ✅ Technical notes file (NOTES.md) for gotchas
3. ✅ Current state table at end of each session
4. ✅ "Initial Approach (Failed)" → "Final Solution" pattern
5. ✅ Explicit file lists with purposes

### Next Session Priorities
1. **O001**: Initialize package (package.json, tsconfig, src/)
2. **O002 + O004 + O005**: Can parallelize after O001!

---

## Quick Start for New Session

```bash
# 1. Navigate to package
cd packages/harness-orchestrator

# 2. Read state (do this every time!)
cat progress.txt | head -100
cat features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes == false) | {id, description}'

# 3. Check NOTES.md for gotchas
cat NOTES.md

# 4. Work on next feature
# ...

# 5. Update status
# - Edit features.json: set passes: true
# - Append to progress.txt
# - Update NOTES.md if learned something new

# 6. Commit ONLY this package
git add packages/harness-orchestrator/
git commit -m "feat(orchestrator): description"
```

---

## Session 2 - 2025-12-16 - O001 Implementation

### Completed ✅

**O001: Package initialization with dependencies**

1. Created `package.json` with:
   - ESM module (`"type": "module"`)
   - Dependencies: commander@13.1.0, chalk@5.4.1, ora@8.1.1, proper-lockfile@4.1.2
   - TypeScript dev dependencies
   - CLI bin entry point

2. Created `tsconfig.json` with:
   - Target: ES2022
   - Module: NodeNext
   - Strict mode enabled
   - Declaration files generated

3. Created full `src/` directory structure:
   ```
   src/
   ├── types.ts           # Core type definitions
   ├── index.ts           # Main exports
   ├── graph/
   │   ├── DependencyGraph.ts   # DAG builder
   │   └── CriticalPath.ts      # Priority calculator
   ├── scheduler/
   │   ├── TaskScheduler.ts     # Ready queue management
   │   └── PriorityQueue.ts     # Min-heap implementation
   ├── agents/
   │   ├── AgentPool.ts         # Multi-agent manager
   │   └── ClaudeAgent.ts       # Single agent wrapper
   ├── state/
   │   ├── StateManager.ts      # Atomic file updates
   │   └── FileLock.ts          # proper-lockfile wrapper
   ├── ai/
   │   └── AIManager.ts         # Complexity analysis
   └── cli/
       └── index.ts             # Commander.js CLI
   ```

4. Verified:
   - TypeScript compiles without errors (`bun run typecheck`)
   - Build succeeds (`bun run build`)
   - CLI works (`node dist/cli/index.js --help`)
   - `analyze` command shows dependency graph
   - `status` command shows progress

### Key Decisions

1. **Went beyond minimum**: Created full src/ structure with placeholder implementations
   - Reason: CLI commands need imports to work
   - Benefit: Next features (O002-O005) just need to enhance existing code

2. **Used proper-lockfile**: Industry-standard file locking
   - Prevents race conditions in multi-agent updates

3. **Implemented Priority Queue**: Min-heap for task scheduling
   - Critical path tasks get higher priority

### Current State

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| O001 | ✅ Complete | Package initialized with full structure |
| O002 | 🔓 Ready | Dependency graph (can start) |
| O003 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O002 |
| O004 | 🔓 Ready | Agent pool (can start) |
| O005 | 🔓 Ready | State manager (can start) |
| O006 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O002-O005 |
| O007 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O006 |
| O008 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O006 |
| O009 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O008 |
| O010 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O006 |

### Next Session Priorities

Now that O001 is complete, these can run **in parallel**:
- **O002**: Enhance DependencyGraph (already has basic implementation)
- **O004**: Enhance AgentPool (already has basic implementation)
- **O005**: Enhance StateManager (already has basic implementation)

### Files Created This Session

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `package.json` | 35 | Package config |
| `tsconfig.json` | 18 | TypeScript config |
| `src/types.ts` | 60 | Core types |
| `src/index.ts` | 16 | Exports |
| `src/graph/DependencyGraph.ts` | 130 | DAG operations |
| `src/graph/CriticalPath.ts` | 100 | Priority calc |
| `src/scheduler/PriorityQueue.ts` | 90 | Min-heap |
| `src/scheduler/TaskScheduler.ts` | 140 | Ready queue |
| `src/agents/ClaudeAgent.ts` | 130 | Agent wrapper |
| `src/agents/AgentPool.ts` | 100 | Pool manager |
| `src/state/FileLock.ts` | 70 | File locking |
| `src/state/StateManager.ts` | 100 | State ops |
| `src/ai/AIManager.ts` | 170 | AI decisions |
| `src/cli/index.ts` | 140 | CLI commands |

---

## Session 3 - 2025-12-16 - O002, O004, O005 Verification

### Context
Continuing from Session 2. Reviewed the implementations created in Session 2 and verified they meet all requirements.

### Verified ✅

**O002: Dependency graph analyzer**
- ✅ Parse features.json into internal model → `DependencyGraph.build()`
- ✅ Build DAG from blockedBy relations → `edges` and `reverseEdges` maps
- ✅ Topological sort for execution order → `topologicalSort()`
- ✅ Identify independent tasks → `getReadyTasks()`
- ✅ Calculate critical path → `CriticalPath.ts` with priority scoring

**O004: Agent pool manager**
- ✅ Spawn Claude processes → `ClaudeAgent.execute()` with spawn()
- ✅ Manage agent lifecycle → `spawnForFeature()`, `killAgent()`, `killAll()`
- ✅ Capture stdout/stderr → Event emissions on 'output' and 'error'
- ✅ Handle crashes gracefully → process.on('error') handling
- ✅ Timeout protection → setTimeout in execute()

**O005: State manager with file locking**
- ✅ Atomic updates → `save()` uses `withLock()`
- ✅ File locking → `FileLock` with proper-lockfile
- ✅ State snapshots → `createSnapshot()`, `restoreSnapshot()`
- ✅ Progress persistence → `load()`, `save()`, `getProgress()`
- ✅ Conflict resolution → File locking prevents concurrent writes

### CLI Verification
```bash
$ node dist/cli/index.js analyze features.json
📊 Dependency Analysis
✓ No cycles detected
Ready to run (parallelizable):
  O002: Dependency graph analyzer [CRITICAL]
  O004: Agent pool manager
  O005: State manager with file locking
Critical Path: O001 → O002 → O003 → O006 → O008 → O009

$ node dist/cli/index.js status features.json
Progress: [████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 40%
  4 completed / 6 pending
```

### Current State

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| O001 | ✅ Complete | Package initialized |
| O002 | ✅ Complete | Graph + critical path working |
| O003 | 🔓 Ready | Next: Task scheduler |

| O008 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O006 |
| O009 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O008 |
| O010 | ⏳ Blocked | Needs O006 |

### Additional Work This Session

After verifying O002, O004, O005, I continued and completed:

**O003: Task scheduler with parallel execution**
- Already fully implemented in Session 2
- Verified: `TaskScheduler` with PriorityQueue, ready queue, in-progress tracking
- All 5 requirements met

**O006: Main orchestrator loop** ← NEWLY IMPLEMENTED
- Created `src/orchestrator/Orchestrator.ts` (220 lines)

### Files Created/Modified This Session

### Key Insight

This demonstrates the value of building a complete skeleton in Session 2.

## Session 4 - 2025-12-16 - O009 & O010 Implementation

### Completed ✅

**O009: Real-time progress dashboard**

Created `src/dashboard/Dashboard.ts` with:

- Read Claude Agent SDK docs: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/overview
- Documented all findings in NOTES.md

**Key Protocol Learnings:**
1. **Two-Agent Architecture**:
   - Initializer Agent: Runs ONCE on first session, creates claude-progress.txt, init.sh, features.json
   - Coding Agent: Runs in ALL subsequent sessions, works on ONE feature per invocation

2. **Session Workflow**:
   - Read git logs + progress files
   - Run init.sh to start dev server
   - Pick highest-priority incomplete feature
   - Implement ONE feature
   - Commit with descriptive messages
   - Update claude-progress.txt

3. **Visual Testing is MANDATORY**:
   - Claude cannot identify non-working features without UI testing
   - Must use Playwright MCP for browser automation

4. **`harness continue` Command**:
   - Core command that implements the protocol
   - Detects first run (no progress.txt) → spawn Initializer
   - Otherwise → spawn Coding Agent
   - Each invocation = one feature completed

**O012: Rename CLI to 'harness' with alias 'h'**
- Updated package.json bin entries
- Updated CLI program name and alias

   - `--init-only` - Only run initializer

{
  "project": "MMS Harness Orchestrator",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "AI Project Manager that orchestrates multiple Claude agents to complete harness tasks in parallel",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16T03",
  "goals": [
    "Analyze task dependencies and identify parallelizable work",
    "Dispatch multiple Claude agents simultaneously",
    "Optimize for quality, speed, and cost",
    "Track progress and handle failures gracefully",
    "Isolate from main project to avoid conflicts"
  ],
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "O001",
      "category": "setup",
      "description": "Package initialization with dependencies",
      "steps": [
        "Create package.json with type: module",
        "Add dependencies: commander, chalk, ora (spinners)",
        "Setup TypeScript configuration",
        "Create src/ directory structure"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Isolated package to avoid conflicts with main project"
    },
    {
      "id": "O002",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Dependency graph analyzer",
      "steps": [
        "Parse features.json into internal model",
        "Build directed acyclic graph (DAG) from blockedBy relations",
        "Implement topological sort for execution order",
        "Identify independent tasks (parallelizable)",
        "Calculate critical path"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O001",
      "notes": "Core algorithm for understanding task relationships"
    },
    {
      "id": "O003",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Task scheduler with parallel execution",
      "steps": [
        "Implement ready queue (tasks with all deps satisfied)",
        "Track in-progress tasks",
        "Limit concurrent agents (configurable)",
        "Handle task completion and unlock dependents",
        "Implement priority scoring (critical path first)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O002",
      "notes": "Scheduler determines what runs when"
    },
    {
      "id": "O004",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Agent pool manager",
      "steps": [
        "Spawn Claude processes with isolated contexts",
        "Manage agent lifecycle (start, monitor, stop)",
        "Capture stdout/stderr for logging",
        "Handle agent crashes gracefully",
        "Implement agent timeout protection"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O001",
      "notes": "Manages multiple Claude instances"
    },
    {
      "id": "O005",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "State manager with file locking",
      "steps": [
        "Atomic updates to features.json",
        "File locking to prevent race conditions",
        "State snapshots for recovery",
        "Progress persistence across restarts",
        "Conflict resolution for concurrent updates"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O001",
      "notes": "Critical for multi-agent coordination"
    },
    {
      "id": "O006",
      "category": "orchestrator",
      "description": "Main orchestrator loop",
      "steps": [
        "Initialize dependency graph",
        "Main loop: schedule -> dispatch -> wait -> update",
        "Handle completion events",
        "Detect and handle deadlocks",
        "Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": ["O002", "O003", "O004", "O005"],
      "notes": "Central coordination logic"
    },
    {
      "id": "O007",
      "category": "intelligence",
      "description": "AI Project Manager agent",
      "steps": [
        "Analyze task complexity for model selection",
        "Generate optimized prompts per task type",
        "Review completed work quality",
        "Suggest task reordering for efficiency",
        "Provide progress summaries"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O006",
      "notes": "The 'brain' that makes smart decisions"
    },
    {
      "id": "O008",
      "category": "cli",
      "description": "CLI interface with commander.js",
      "steps": [
        "orchestrate command - run full orchestration",
        "analyze command - show dependency graph",
        "status command - show current progress",
        "Options: --max-agents, --model, --dry-run",
        "Colored output with chalk"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O006",
      "notes": "User-friendly command line interface"
    },
    {
      "id": "O009",
      "category": "monitoring",
      "description": "Real-time TERMINAL dashboard (NOT web UI)",
      "steps": [
        "Terminal UI using chalk/ora (command line only)",
        "Progress bars per feature rendered in terminal",
        "Cost tracking displayed in terminal output",
        "Time estimates shown in terminal",
        "Log streaming from agents to stdout"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O008",
      "notes": "CLARIFICATION: This is a TERMINAL-BASED dashboard (src/dashboard/Dashboard.ts) using chalk for colors. NOT a web dashboard with Astro/React. It renders progress directly in the command line when running 'harness orchestrate --dashboard' or 'harness dashboard'."
    },
    {
      "id": "O010",
      "category": "quality",
      "description": "Verification and rollback system",
      "steps": [
        "Git branch per feature for isolation",
        "Automated verification of passes criteria",
        "Rollback failed features",
        "Merge successful features to main",
        "Conflict resolution strategy"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O006",
      "notes": "GitManager + Verifier classes with CLI commands (verify, rollback, branches)"
    },
    {
      "id": "O011",
      "category": "bugfix",
      "description": "Commit pending code review fixes",
      "steps": [
        "Commit ClaudeAgent.ts fix (JSON.stringify double quotes)",
        "Commit cli/index.ts fix (async/await in verify and rollback)",
        "Commit Verifier.ts fix (resolveConflicts dead code)",
        "Commit Dashboard.ts fix (maxAgents hardcoded)",
        "Commit README.md (new documentation)",
        "Commit progress.txt updates"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O010",
      "notes": "Committed 9d3d27b with all fixes + README.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "O012",
      "category": "cli",
      "description": "Rename CLI to 'harness' with alias 'h'",
      "steps": [
        "Update package.json bin to 'harness' and 'h'",
        "Update README.md with new command names",
        "Re-link globally with bun link",
        "Test both 'harness' and 'h' commands work"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O011",
      "notes": "User requested simpler command name"
    },
    {
      "id": "O013",
      "category": "cli",
      "description": "Make features.json path optional with default",
      "steps": [
        "Change all commands from <features-path> to [features-path]",
        "Default to 'features.json' in current directory",
        "Update help text to reflect optional parameter",
        "Test all commands work without path argument"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O011",
      "notes": "Reduce typing for common case"
    },
    {
      "id": "O014",
      "category": "research",
      "description": "Study Anthropic Harness protocol via web search",
      "steps": [
        "WebSearch 'Anthropic harness agents 2025'",
        "Read official Anthropic documentation on harnesses",
        "Understand the complete harness protocol",
        "Document findings in NOTES.md",
        "Compare with current root implementation"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O011",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Documented in NOTES.md. Key learnings: Two-agent architecture (Initializer + Coding), claude-progress.txt for state, ONE feature per session, visual testing mandatory, Agent SDK with hooks/MCP/sessions"
    },
    {
      "id": "O015",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement 'harness init' command",
      "steps": [
        "Create init command that generates harness structure",
        "Generate features.json template",
        "Generate progress.txt / claude-progress.txt template",
        "Generate CLAUDE.md template",
        "Generate .claude/settings.json",
        "Generate .claude/hooks/ with standard hooks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O014",
      "notes": "Bootstrap new harness projects with full structure"
    },
    {
      "id": "O016",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement hooks system matching root .claude/hooks/",
      "steps": [
        "Study root hooks: session-start.sh, end-of-turn-check.sh, protect-package-json.sh, after-write.sh, task-type-detector.py",
        "Implement hook templates in CLI",
        "Ensure hooks enforce harness protocol",
        "Ensure hooks prevent commits without proper workflow",
        "Ensure hooks trigger visual testing (MCP Playwright)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O014",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Created HookTemplates.ts with all 4 hooks (session-start, end-of-turn-check, protect-package-json, after-write). Integrated into init command."
    },
    {
      "id": "O017",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement progress.txt / claude-progress.txt management",
      "steps": [
        "Add 'harness session' command to create new session entry",
        "Add 'harness log' command to append to progress",
        "Parse and display progress history",
        "Integrate with status command"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O015",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Added session, log, and progress commands. Session auto-detects session number and includes feature status."
    },
    {
      "id": "O018",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement visual testing integration (MCP Playwright)",
      "steps": [
        "Study how root harness uses MCP Playwright for visual tests",
        "Add visual test triggers to hooks",
        "Ensure features cannot be marked complete without visual verification",
        "Document visual testing protocol in generated CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O016",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Created src/testing/VisualTesting.ts with: generateVisualTestCommands(), generateQAChecklist(), requiresVisualTesting(), getMcpConfig(). Added CLI commands: 'harness test' (generate Playwright MCP commands), 'harness test --checklist' (QA checklist), 'harness test --setup' (generate .mcp.json)."
    },
    {
      "id": "O019",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement commit workflow enforcement",
      "steps": [
        "Study how root hooks handle git commits",
        "Ensure commits only happen after proper workflow",
        "Prevent commits without feature completion",
        "Add commit message templates following harness protocol"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "O016",
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Created src/workflow/CommitWorkflow.ts with: validateCommit(), checkFeatureReadyForCommit(), generateGitCommitCommand(), getStagedFiles(). Added CLI commands: 'harness commit -f <id>' (validate and prepare commit), 'harness pre-commit' (for git hooks). Validates: passes:true in features.json, progress updated, no sensitive files, staged changes."
    },
    {
      "id": "O020",
      "category": "testing",
      "description": "E2E test: Validate harness-orchestrator CLI using harness-dashboard as test subject",
      "steps": [
        "Use packages/harness-dashboard as a REAL PROJECT to test our CLI",
        "Run 'harness status' on harness-dashboard and verify output",
        "Run 'harness analyze' on harness-dashboard and verify dependency graph",
        "Run 'harness verify' on harness-dashboard and verify checks work",
        "Test hooks execute properly when running commands",
        "Document any CLI bugs or issues found during testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": ["O018", "O019"],
      "notes": "CLARIFICATION: This is NOT about building harness-dashboard. harness-dashboard is a SEPARATE project (an Astro/React web dashboard) that happens to use the harness pattern. We use it as a TEST SUBJECT to validate that our harness-orchestrator CLI works correctly on real projects. TESTED: status (32 features displayed), analyze (critical path D001→D025, no cycles), test --checklist (auto-selected D001), verify (git status shown). All commands work correctly."
    },
    {
      "id": "O021",
      "category": "parity",
      "description": "Implement 'harness continue' command per Anthropic protocol",
      "steps": [
        "Detect if first run (no progress.txt) → spawn Initializer Agent",
        "If progress.txt exists → spawn Coding Agent",
        "Coding Agent: read state, pick highest-priority incomplete feature",
        "Work on ONE feature per invocation (prevents context overflow)",
        "Commit to git with descriptive messages",
        "Update claude-progress.txt before session end",
        "Integrate with Playwright MCP for visual testing",
        "Use --no-session-persistence for fresh context each run"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": ["O015", "O016"],
      "notes": "COMPLETED: Two-Agent Architecture implemented. Initializer creates progress.txt and runs init.sh. Coding Agent uses DependencyGraph + CriticalPath to pick next feature and spawns Claude."
    }
  ],
  "architecture": {
    "overview": "Multi-agent orchestration system using Claude Code CLI",
    "components": {
      "DependencyGraph": "Analyzes blockedBy relations, finds parallel tasks",
      "TaskScheduler": "Manages ready queue, tracks in-progress work",
      "AgentPool": "Spawns and manages Claude processes",
      "StateManager": "Atomic state updates with file locking",
      "Orchestrator": "Main loop coordinating all components",
      "AIManager": "Makes intelligent decisions about task execution"
    },
    "dataFlow": [
      "1. Load features.json -> Build DAG",
      "2. Identify ready tasks (no pending deps)",
      "3. Score and prioritize (critical path)",
      "4. Dispatch to available agents",
      "5. Monitor completion, update state",
      "6. Unlock dependent tasks, repeat"
    ]
  },
  "considerations": {
    "isolation": "Run in packages/harness-orchestrator to avoid conflicts",
    "atomicity": "Use file locking for features.json updates",
    "recovery": "Snapshot state before each batch for rollback",
    "cost": "Track API usage, use cheaper models for simple tasks",
    "quality": "Verify each feature before marking complete"
  }
}

        "3. IMPLEMENT BasicChecker class with constructor(projectPath: string)",

        "7. ADD CHECK: checkInitSh() - verify init.sh exists (warning if missing, not error)",
        "8. ADD CHECK: checkHooksDir() - verify .claude/hooks/ exists with at least one .sh file, check .claude/settings.json exists",
        "9. ADD CHECK: checkGitRepo() - verify .git/ directory exists (error if missing)",
        "10. ADD CHECK: checkHarnessKb() - verify .harness/kb/ exists (info only, used by AI checks)",
        "11. IMPLEMENT runAllChecks(): Promise<CheckResult[]> that runs all checks and returns array",
        "12. ADD CLI: 'harness doctor [path]' command that runs BasicChecker and displays results with chalk colors",

      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "O020",
      "notes": "CONTEXT: This is the FIRST part of the 'harness doctor' command. It performs PROGRAMMATIC checks only - no AI is involved. REFERENCE FILES: See src/verification/Verifier.ts for similar pattern of check results. See src/cli/index.ts for CLI command structure with commander.js and chalk."
    },

      "steps": [

        "2. IMPLEMENT interface AICheckResult: { valid: boolean, score: number (0-100), issues: Array<{ severity: 'error'|'warning'|'info', message: string, fix: string }> }",
        "3. IMPLEMENT class AIChecker with constructor(projectPath: string, knowledgeManager: KnowledgeManager)",
        "4. IMPLEMENT private readHarnessFiles(): Promise<string> - read features.json, claude-progress.txt, CLAUDE.md and format as XML: <harness-files><file path=\"features.json\">...</file></harness-files>",
        "5. IMPLEMENT checkStructure(): Promise<AICheckResult>",
        "6. IN checkStructure: read KB with this.knowledgeManager.readForAI()",
        "7. IN checkStructure: build prompt with structure validation criteria",
        "8. PROMPT TEMPLATE: 'You are a harness structure validator. Using the knowledge base provided, verify this harness follows Anthropic recommended patterns.\\n<knowledge-base>{kb}</knowledge-base>\\n<harness-files>{files}</harness-files>\\nValidate: 1. Two-Agent Architecture compliance 2. Session workflow correctness 3. Hook configuration 4. CLAUDE.md completeness\\nOutput JSON only: { \"valid\": boolean, \"issues\": [...], \"score\": number }'",
        "9. SPAWN Claude using child_process with: claude --print -p 'prompt' (similar to how 'harness continue' spawns Claude)",
        "10. PARSE JSON response from Claude output",
        "11. HANDLE errors gracefully (timeout, invalid JSON, Claude not installed)",
        "12. DISPLAY results: '🔍 AI Structure Verification\\nScore: 92/100\\n✓ Two-Agent Architecture: OK\\n⚠️ Hooks: Missing end-of-turn-check\\n...'"

    "overview": "Multi-agent orchestration system using Claude Code CLI",
    "components": {
      "DependencyGraph": "Analyzes blockedBy relations, finds parallel tasks",
      "TaskScheduler": "Manages ready queue, tracks in-progress work",
      "AgentPool": "Spawns and manages Claude processes",
      "StateManager": "Atomic state updates with file locking",
      "Orchestrator": "Main loop coordinating all components",
      "AIManager": "Makes intelligent decisions about task execution"
    },
    "dataFlow": [
      "1. Load features.json -> Build DAG",
      "2. Identify ready tasks (no pending deps)",
      "3. Score and prioritize (critical path)",
      "4. Dispatch to available agents",
      "5. Monitor completion, update state",
      "6. Unlock dependent tasks, repeat"
    ]

    "recovery": "Snapshot state before each batch for rollback",
    "cost": "Track API usage, use cheaper models for simple tasks",
    "quality": "Verify each feature before marking complete"
  }
}
